---
title: "Official R Guides"
format: html
---

Here are a collection of R cookbooks and guides that have informed my own workflows stored in this repository.

-   Learning Statistics with R [@Navarro] - unbelievably helpful! ⭐⭐⭐

-   The R Cookbook [@LongTeetor] - basics ⭐⭐

-   The R Graphics Cookbook [@Chang] - basics of figures ⭐⭐

-   Doing Meta-Analysis in R: A Hands-On Guide [@HarrerCuijpersFurukawa] - supremely useful for understanding three-level random-effects meta-analysis ⭐⭐⭐

-   And [here](https://github.com/qinwf/awesome-R) you can find a list of fantastic R packages under a number of categories. For example, you might be interested in using R for text mining, or you might want to conduct Bayesian analysis.
